name: Comal River Tubing
description: Float the crystal-clear spring-fed Comal River, the shortest river in Texas. Perfect for a relaxing 2-hour journey with access points throughout the city.

name: Guadalupe River Adventures
description: Kayaking, tubing, and paddleboarding on the Guadalupe River. Full-day trips with beautiful Hill Country scenery.

name: Schlitterbahn Waterpark
description: One of the best waterparks in the country, powered by natural spring water from the Comal River. Multiple water coasters, lazy rivers, and slides.

name: Gruene Hall
description: Texas' oldest continually operating dance hall, built in 1878. Live music every night, from up-and-coming artists to national acts.

name: Gruene Historic District
description: Shop, dine, and explore charming storefronts in this preserved 1800s German settlement. Wine tasting rooms, antique shops, and the famous Gristmill restaurant.

name: Natural Bridge Caverns
description: The largest commercial caverns in Texas, 180 feet underground. Discovery Tour shows massive formations, plus zip lines and a maze above ground.

name: Natural Bridge Wildlife Ranch
description: A 500-acre drive-through safari park with over 500 animals. Feed giraffes, see zebras, and explore walkabout exhibits.

name: Landa Park
description: A 51-acre city park along the Comal River with a spring-fed pool, miniature train, paddle boats, and walking trails. Free admission.

name: The Cliff Carver Alpine Coaster
description: Texas' first alpine coaster. A gravity-propelled sled zooms through the Hill Country hillside at up to 25mph.

name: Wurstfest
description: The annual 10-day salute to sausage drawing 240,000+ visitors. German food, 40+ bands on 5 stages, carnival rides, and beer. Every November.

name: Gruene Music & Wine Festival
description: Four-day celebration featuring 50+ Texas wineries and breweries with live music in the Gruene Historic District.
